commonMedications.name: Semaglutida / GLP-1 (Ozempic) [P1]
commonMedications.coverageNote: Available at Farmacias del Ahorro, Similares. No IMSS coverage for expats typically.

commonMedications.name: Levotiroxina [P1]
commonMedications.coverageNote: Very inexpensive generic at Farmacia Similares

medicine.pharmacyAccess: Farmacias del Ahorro, Farmacias Similares (Dr. Simi — very affordable generics), Farmacias Guadalajara, Walmart pharmacy. Many medications available without prescription.
medicine.notes: Mexico offers very affordable generic medications. Farmacia Similares provides interchangeable generics at 50-80% below brand prices. IMSS (public insurance) available to residents for ~$500/yr but quality varies. Most expats use private pharmacies. GLP-1 drugs are expensive imports.

plans.name: Google Fi Flexible (US Number)
plans.notes: Line 1 $20 + Line 2 $15 = $35 base. Data $10/GB × 3GB = $30. Works in Mexico at same rate. Keeps US number for banking/2FA.

plans.name: Telcel Postpaid
plans.notes: 2 lines ~MX$150/line (~$8). Telcel has best coverage. IVA included.

items.name: Ground Beef / Turkey
items.notes: Lean ground for dog food base

items.name: Fish (local catch)
items.notes: Fresh local fish often cheaper than US

items.name: Coffee (ground/beans)
items.notes: Excellent local coffee in most LATAM countries

items.name: Premium Dog Food (kibble)
items.notes: Imported premium brands cost more; local brands available

breakdown.notes: No rental tax in Mexico. Predial (property tax) is landlord responsibility. Water may be included.

housing.taxNotes: No VAT/IVA on residential rent. Predial property tax paid by owner. Foreign renters can sign contracts in pesos or USD.
housing.leaseTerms: 12-month lease standard. Security deposit typically 1 month rent. Some landlords require aval (guarantor) or additional deposit for foreigners.
housing.marketNotes: Expat-popular areas command premium rents. Furnished rentals common and preferred by expats. Negotiate directly with owners for best rates.

stores.name: Soriana
stores.notes: Major Mexican supermarket chain with competitive prices

stores.name: Chedraui
stores.notes: Large supermarket chain, good for bulk staples

stores.name: Walmart Mexico
stores.notes: Familiar format with local and imported products

stores.name: La Comer
stores.notes: Mid-to-high range, good produce and deli

stores.name: Costco Mexico
stores.notes: Bulk buying, imported American brands, membership required

healthcare.coverageSummary: IMSS voluntary enrollment ~$400/year for permanent residents. Private insurance $150-300/mo per person depending on age/conditions. Out-of-pocket GP visits $20-40.

gpVisit.notes: GP consultations very affordable; many doctors trained in US/EU

specialist.notes: Specialist visits $50-100 without insurance. Major cities have excellent specialists.

privateInsurance.notes: GNP Seguros, AXA Mexico, or international plans. Pre-existing conditions may be excluded first year.

emergency.notes: Private hospital ER visit $200-800 without insurance. IMSS free with enrollment.

dental.notes: Dental tourism hub. Prices 50-70% less than US.

healthcare.notes: Mexico has world-class private hospitals in major cities. IMSS provides basic coverage. Many expats use combination of IMSS + private insurance.

womensCut.notes: Salons range from basic to upscale

gymMonthly.notes: Smart Fit, Sport City available in Playa del Carmen, Mexico

seniorDiscounts.programName: INAPAM (Instituto Nacional de las Personas Adultas Mayores)

discounts.name: INAPAM Card
discounts.description: Free government-issued senior card for adults 60+. Provides 10-50% discounts on transportation, restaurants, hotels, medicine, and cultural events.

discounts.name: Public Transit Discount
discounts.description: INAPAM cardholders get 50% off public transit, intercity buses, and some domestic flights.

discounts.name: Healthcare Discounts
discounts.description: IMSS enrollment available. INAPAM card provides discounts at pharmacies (5-15%) and some private clinics.

discounts.name: Cultural & Recreation
discounts.description: Free or reduced entry to museums, archaeological sites, national parks, and cultural events with INAPAM card.

discounts.name: Property Tax Reduction
discounts.description: Some municipalities offer predial (property tax) reductions for seniors. Varies by state.

discounts.name: Utility Discounts
discounts.description: CFE (electricity) offers tariff reductions for seniors in some states. Water discounts available in some municipalities.

monthlyPass.notes: Metro very affordable ($0.25 USD). INAPAM card widely accepted for senior fares.

publicTransit.coverage: Metro and Metrobus in major cities. Colectivos and combis for local transport.
publicTransit.seniorDiscount: INAPAM card gives free or reduced transit for 60+. Free Metro in CDMX.

rideShare.availability: Uber, DiDi, and InDriver widely available. Very affordable.
rideShare.notes: Ride-share widely used in Playa Del Carmen. Negotiate taxi fares before riding if no meter.

registration.notes: Vehicle registration through local transit authority in Playa Del Carmen.

insurance.notes: Liability insurance mandatory. International driving permit accepted for first 90 days.

fuel.notes: Fuel prices vary by region. Government may regulate prices.

parking.notes: Street parking available in Playa Del Carmen. Malls and supermarkets usually have free parking.

tolls.notes: Toll roads on major highways between cities.

carOwnership.seniorDiscounts: INAPAM discounts on some auto services. Verificacion vehicular required in some states.

transportation.walkability: Good in city centers and colonial towns. Varies by location.

cycling.infrastructure: Bicycle infrastructure varies in Playa Del Carmen. Urban areas gradually improving cycling lanes.
cycling.notes: Heat and terrain may limit cycling as primary transport.

electricVehicle.purchaseNotes: Growing EV market. Tesla, BYD, MG available. Limited charging outside major cities. Import costs may apply.
electricVehicle.incentiveNotes: Some states offer EV incentives. Federal government promoting electric mobility.

chargingHome.notes: Home charging feasible in houses and some apartments in Playa Del Carmen.

chargingPublic.notes: Public charging network growing in Mexico. Check availability in Playa Del Carmen.

insurance.notes: EV insurance through major local providers.

maintenance.notes: EV service availability growing. Authorized dealers in major cities.

electricVehicle.batteryWarranty: Manufacturer standard warranty. Service access best in major cities.
electricVehicle.depreciationNotes: EV resale market developing in Mexico. Larger cities have better resale prospects.
electricVehicle.totalMonthlyNotes: Includes: charging, insurance, maintenance (monthly avg), registration. Excludes: purchase/loan payment, depreciation.
